channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Recent Analyst Coverage and Price Target Updates for Tech and Software Companies
item.description: Stay up-to-date with the latest analyst coverage and price target updates in the tech and software sector, including companies like Palantir, Reddit, and Nutanix.
item.dc:creator: Tiernan Ray, Fast Company

item.title: Texting and chat apps aren’t always secure. Use these encrypted platforms instead
item.description: After a sprawling hacking campaign exposed the communications of an unknown number of Americans, U.S. cybersecurity officials are advising people to use encryption in their communications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Apple rumored to have started production on the iPhone 17 Air
item.description: Moves are now underway to start production on the iPhone 17 Air, which is apparently replacing the iPhone 16 Plus.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Your phone got hacked and now someone's got control of it. What to do immediately
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson lays out the immediate steps you should take if your phone has been hacked and your personal information becomes vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal appeals court blocks push to delay January TikTok ban
item.description: A federal appeals court in Washington denied a request from the social platform TikTok that would effectively delay the ban of the app next month. The rejection comes after the company sought to stop the ban Monday, asking the U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Trump hosts Apple CEO at Mar-a-Lago as big tech leaders continue outreach to president-elect
item.description: Donald Trump is hosting Apple CEO Tim Cook for a Friday evening dinner at the president-elect’s Mar-a-Lago resort.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Court denies TikTok's request to halt enforcement of potential US ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday left in place a mid-January deadline in a federal law requiring TikTok to be sold or face a ban in the United States, rejecting a request made by the company to halt enforcement until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge of the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Strengthening the Role of the White House Office of the National Cyber Director
item.description: Cyber policy experts advocate for a stronger national cyber director, recommending legislative action to clarify the role and responsibilities within the US government.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: For December’s Patch Tuesday, 74 updates and a zero-day fix for Windows
item.description: Microsoft released 74 updates in its December Patch Tuesday update, with patches for Windows, Office and Edge — but none for Microsoft Exchange Server or SQL server.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: NotebookLM Plus is now available to Google Workspace customers
item.description: A new premium version of Google’s NotebookLM AI assistant is now available to Google Workspace customers. Google unveiled NotebookLM last year, initially under the name Project Tailwind, and began testing the AI-powered “notebook” with select users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: YouTube is taking over TV
item.description: YouTube was launched in February 2005 and soon adopted a simple motto: “Broadcast Yourself.” While we initially watched on PCs, and then smartphones, it seems that these days an increasing number of us are engaging with YouTube content on our televisions.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: Sophia, a world-renowned robot, has been the center of attention at an Artificial Intelligence and Innovation fair in Zimbabwe this week.
item.dc:creator: By Farai Mutsaka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Visual Studio Code adds overtype mode, paste with imports
item.description: Visual Studio Code 1.96, the latest version of Microsoft’s popular code editor, has arrived with an overtype mode and a “paste with imports” feature for JavaScript and TypeScript.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: By 2028, 30% of Fortune 500 companies could use AI-only service channels
item.description: As businesses race toward digital transformation, Gartner has forecasted a game-changing shift in customer service strategies for Fortune 500 companies.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Kazakhstan’s Carpet CCTV: Pioneering the future of AI-powered public safety
item.description: In a world where technology increasingly shapes how cities manage safety and security, Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs is leading the way with its groundbreaking “Carpet CCTV” project.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Dashboards, sharing threat intelligence help USDA improve cybersecurity response
item.description: When it comes to cybersecurity, the more data you have, the easier it is to identify anomalies and stop adversaries, shares Agriculture CISO Ignatius Liberto. The post Dashboards, sharing threat intelligence help USDA improve cybersecurity response first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: 3 takeaways from the Ultralytics AI Python library hack
item.description: When attackers compromised Ultralytics YOLO, a popular real-time object detection machine-learning package for Python, most assumed the Python Package Index, or PyPI, must be the point of failure. That made sense because the tampered software artifact was first found on PyPI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Boosting Cyprus' defense is key so close to the war-wracked Mideast, defense minister says
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Bolstering Cyprus ' defense capacity is critical for the east Mediterranean island nation so close to the war-wrecked Middle East, the country's defense minister said Tuesday. The priority for Cyprus is to procure better defense systems, said Defense Minister Vasilis Palmas.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nvidia's stock dips after China opens probe of the AI chip company for violating anti-monopoly laws
item.description: Shares of Nvidia fell Monday after China said it is investigating the high-flying U.S. microchip company over suspected violations of Chinese anti-monopoly laws.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: California to consider requiring mental health warnings on social media sites
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California, home to some of the largest technology companies in the world, would be the first U.S. state to require mental health warning labels on social media sites if lawmakers pass a bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Protecting yourself from Microsoft tech support scams
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says scammers exploit fears, falsely claim computer issues to access your personal data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Don't get fooled by fake phone updates and notifications
item.description: Updates to apps are essential for maintaining the performance and security of your devices, including bug fixes, new features and security patches.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
